_readme.what_this_is: A labeled corpus for measuring the Foresight reasoning verifier. Each case is a minimal known-good or known-bad implementation of one checkpoint, with a gold level a competent reviewer would assign. The harness (run-eval.mjs) runs a verifier against these and reports precision/recall — especially the false-green rate (calling a bad implementation shippable), the dangerous error.
_readme.labels: gold_level uses the 3/6/9 scale. 3 = crude/risky (the vulnerability is present). 6 = solid/shippable (the property holds). shippable = level >= 6.
_readme.ids: checkpoint ids are library ids. The corpus is ARCHETYPE-AGNOSTIC — it grades checkpoint definitions (loaded from library/checkpoints/), so one corpus covers checkpoints used by ecommerce, SaaS, portfolio, and any future archetype. Fixture directory names mirror the id.
_readme.wall: Fixtures are synthetic and contain no real project code — PATTERN-level and fully shareable. Point the verifier at a real repo separately.
_readme.coverage: Covers the high-stakes backbone checkpoints across the first three archetypes (ecommerce, SaaS, portfolio/web). Design checkpoints need rendered output and aren't fixtured as static snippets yet. Expand with more cases (and 9-level fixtures) over time.

cases.label: bad
cases.note: availability check and decrement are separate non-atomic steps; race window oversells

cases.label: good
cases.note: atomic conditional reservation committed before the payment intent, with an expiring hold

cases.label: bad
cases.note: no idempotency key; webhook reprocesses on every redelivery

cases.label: good
cases.note: idempotency key on creation; webhook dedupes by provider event id

cases.label: bad
cases.note: record marked paid from the client redirect, before provider confirmation

cases.label: good
cases.note: paid set only from a verified webhook via a state machine, plus reconciliation

cases.label: bad
cases.note: raw PAN posted to first-party backend; secret key in client config

cases.label: good
cases.note: provider token only; secret key server-side via env

cases.label: bad
cases.note: webhook trusts the body with no signature verification

cases.label: good
cases.note: verifies provider signature against the raw body before mutating state

cases.label: bad
cases.note: fetch by id alone — IDOR

cases.label: good
cases.note: fetch filtered by authenticated owner id

cases.label: bad
cases.note: non-expiring session, client-only logout, guessable reset token

cases.label: good
cases.note: random short-lived sessions, server-side revocation, single-use expiring reset tokens

cases.label: bad
cases.note: query by id alone — crosses the tenant boundary

cases.label: good
cases.note: every query scoped by tenant id from the session

cases.label: bad
cases.note: gate trusts a client header / a flag set once at signup

cases.label: good
cases.note: entitlement from server-side subscription state updated by webhooks

cases.label: bad
cases.note: only create/cancel; failed renewal leaves status active forever

cases.label: good
cases.note: trial-end, dunning, proration, cancel-at-period-end handled

cases.label: bad
cases.note: render-blocking scripts, unsized eager images, no lazy-loading

cases.label: good
cases.note: sized/compressed/lazy images, deferred scripts, preloaded hero

cases.label: bad
cases.note: generic title, no description, no Open Graph, div soup

cases.label: good
cases.note: unique title + description, Open Graph, semantic landmarks

cases.label: bad
cases.note: trusts the client, no validation or spam protection

cases.label: good
cases.note: server-side validation + honeypot + rate limit

cases.label: bad
cases.note: SELECT availability then a separate unconditional UPDATE decrement: read-then-write TOCTOU race window

cases.label: bad
cases.note: stock decremented only after the payment intent confirms, so concurrent buyers oversell during payment

cases.label: bad
cases.note: UPDATE decrements with only WHERE id=? and no stock>=qty guard, driving stock negative under concurrency

cases.label: good
cases.note: single atomic conditional UPDATE SET stock=stock-qty WHERE id=? AND stock>=qty, hold committed before the intent

cases.label: good
cases.note: SELECT FOR UPDATE row lock in a txn to decrement + create hold, commit, then create the intent outside the txn

cases.label: bad
cases.note: idempotency key on creation but webhook mutates state on every delivery with no dedupe (redelivery double-fulfills)

cases.label: bad
cases.note: webhook dedupes by orderId instead of provider event id, dropping distinct legitimate events

cases.label: bad
cases.note: retry wrapper re-calls paymentIntents.create on network error with no idempotency key (transient failure double-creates)

cases.label: good
cases.note: insert-first exactly-once: inserts provider event id into a unique-constraint table and bails on failure, plus key on creation

cases.label: good
cases.note: idempotency key on creation plus webhook that checks-and-records processed event id in a transaction before mutating

cases.label: bad
cases.note: status set to paid on intent creation, not on provider confirmation; reconcile stub only reads, never transitions

cases.label: bad
cases.note: webhook writes paid with no current-state guard, so replayed/out-of-order events regress fulfilled/refunded orders

cases.label: bad
cases.note: capture at provider with no local record write, and a separate path creates a paid record with no capture — money/records diverge

cases.label: good
cases.note: idempotent webhook-driven paid transition guarded by pending status, ignores non-pending/terminal orders

cases.label: good
cases.note: guarded webhook paid transition plus reconciliation comparing provider state to local and flagging mismatches

cases.label: bad
cases.note: Stripe secret key exposed to the client via NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_SECRET_KEY in browser code

cases.label: bad
cases.note: full PAN and CVV logged to console and persisted to the database

cases.label: bad
cases.note: first-party form POSTs raw card number/cvv/exp to the app's own backend endpoint

cases.label: good
cases.note: client-side Elements tokenization; only the paymentMethod id reaches the backend

cases.label: good
cases.note: provider-hosted Checkout session created server-side with the secret from env; no card fields in code

cases.label: bad
cases.note: verifies the signature against JSON.stringify of the parsed body, not the raw request bytes

cases.label: bad
cases.note: authenticates via shared-secret query-param equality instead of a payload signature

cases.label: bad
cases.note: compares a self-computed HMAC with naive === equality, timing-unsafe, no provider verify

cases.label: good
cases.note: uses the Stripe SDK constructEvent on the raw body + signing secret, rejects before any state change

cases.label: good
cases.note: manual HMAC-SHA256 over the raw body compared with crypto.timingSafeEqual against the signature header

cases.label: bad
cases.note: authenticates the session but fetches the record by id alone with no ownership filter

cases.label: bad
cases.note: read path scoped by userId but a sibling update mutates by id alone (write path is the hole)

cases.label: bad
cases.note: scopes the query by a client-supplied req.query.userId instead of the session identity

cases.label: good
cases.note: findFirst scoped by id AND session.userId, returns 404 on mismatch

cases.label: good
cases.note: fetch then an explicit userId ownership assertion applied on both read and delete

cases.label: bad
cases.note: tenant id scoping the query is read from req.body instead of the session

cases.label: bad
cases.note: join filters only the primary table by tenant; joined rows are not tenant-scoped and leak across tenants

cases.label: bad
cases.note: user-facing export uses an adminDb raw query that bypasses the tenant scoping applied elsewhere

cases.label: good
cases.note: every query including the joined table is scoped by the session tenantId

cases.label: good
cases.note: a repository wrapper injects the session tenantId into every query so call sites can't forget isolation

cases.label: bad
cases.note: gate trusts client-controllable JWT claims (session.claims.plan) instead of server-side subscription state

cases.label: bad
cases.note: plan/seats flag written on the user record at signup and never updated, so it goes stale on lapse/cancel

cases.label: bad
cases.note: gate checks only that a subscription row exists, not status/period, so canceled/expired subs still pass

cases.label: good
cases.note: entitlement requires status==active AND now<currentPeriodEnd from webhook-updated subscription state

cases.label: good
cases.note: resolveEntitlements helper returns entitlements only for an active, in-period subscription; checks call it each time

cases.label: bad
cases.note: SQL injection: user query interpolated into raw SQL via template literal

cases.label: bad
cases.note: NoSQL injection: req.body passed directly as a Mongo filter (operator injection)

cases.label: bad
cases.note: XSS: user-supplied text assigned to innerHTML without encoding/sanitization

cases.label: bad
cases.note: OS command injection: user filename interpolated into an exec shell string

cases.label: good
cases.note: parameterized query: user input bound as a $1 placeholder, never concatenated

cases.label: good
cases.note: safe output: allowlist sanitization (DOMPurify) before insertion

cases.label: good
cases.note: execFile with an argument array (no shell) plus strict filename allowlist

cases.label: bad
cases.note: real-looking API key hardcoded as a string literal and used directly

cases.label: bad
cases.note: secret leaked to the client bundle via a NEXT_PUBLIC_ env var in a browser component

cases.label: bad
cases.note: apiKey/dbPassword committed as literals in an in-repo config object that is imported

cases.label: bad
cases.note: secret written to logs and returned in the API response body

cases.label: good
cases.note: secret read from process.env server-side with a startup check; never sent to the client

cases.label: good
cases.note: client calls own server route; server-only handler reads the key from env

cases.label: good
cases.note: secret pulled from a secret manager / injected env; repo ships only .env.example placeholders

cases.label: bad
cases.note: passwords stored in plaintext; login compares plaintext

cases.label: bad
cases.note: fast unsalted sha256 hash of the password

cases.label: bad
cases.note: reversible AES encryption with a stored key, decrypted on login

cases.label: bad
cases.note: home-rolled sha256(globalSalt+password) with a single server-wide salt

cases.label: good
cases.note: bcrypt with a per-user salt (cost 12) and bcrypt.compare

cases.label: good
cases.note: argon2id with recommended memory/time/parallelism parameters

cases.label: good
cases.note: scrypt with a random per-user salt and crypto.timingSafeEqual comparison
